identity.principle: Mission Control tells the truth about itself. Never inflate completion.

questions.q: Can a first-time visitor understand the issue?
Can a local volunteer teach others?

phases.title: Foundation & Governance
phases.notes: Constitution, Mission Control, and Phase Registry locked. Master Build Plan documented but step tracking early.

phases.title: Information Architecture
phases.notes: Hall routes and depth model exist. Formal IA doc and search not started.

phases.title: Historical Foundation
phases.notes: Hall 1 (Before Citizens United) is L1 stub only. Timeline preview on homepage.

phases.title: The Case
phases.notes: Hall 2 route exists. No oral argument or opinion walkthrough yet.

phases.title: Constitutional Principles
phases.notes: Hall 3 L1 stub. Precedent chain not written.

phases.title: Impact Analysis
phases.notes: Halls 4â€“5 are stubs. No FEC/spending data integrated.

phases.title: Data & Visualization
phases.notes: Chart definitions in inventories only. No static or interactive charts.

phases.title: Debate & Perspectives
phases.notes: Debate hall L1 only. Opposition/supporter research not collected.

phases.title: Reform & Current Developments
phases.notes: Montana I-194 and Hawaii Act 11 live in Hall 7. Reform hall mostly stub.

phases.title: Civic Action Platform
phases.notes: Action Hub and forms live. Workspaces stubbed. Participant backend blocked.

phases.title: Community Leadership
phases.notes: Educate funnel and Teach This Locally page exist. No facilitator handbook.

phases.title: Technical Platform
phases.notes: Static site on GitHub/Netlify. No search, security review, or full a11y audit.

phases.title: Research Library
phases.notes: Inventories and claims ledger started. Library page is early scaffold.

phases.title: Quality Assurance
phases.notes: Editorial checklist exists. No systematic QA pass.

phases.title: Launch & Growth
phases.notes: Site is live in early-foundation mode. Not ready for broad public education launch.

steps.title: Site mission: deep public understanding of Citizens United
Site promise: plain language + deep research
Success metric: education leads + civic participation
Test production deploy forms Action Hub

builds.title: Citizens United Facts Framework
builds.purpose: Canonical Facts & Evidence Architecture v1.0 — foundation for all educational content
builds.summary: 13 facts in 6 categories; 4 confidence levels; L1–L4 presentation; EV/KG integration; claims migrated.
decisions_made: FACT-1000 through FACT-6000 category system
Four confidence levels: confirmed, strongly_supported, context_dependent, under_review
Facts trace to Evidence Registry (EV-*) and Knowledge Graph (KG-*)
Claims ledger linked via fact_id — 3 claims migrated
Build #19: Brand & Identity System before mass content
risks: Only 13 of ~200 v1 target facts cataloged — gaps documented

builds.title: Component Architecture & Design System Inventory
builds.purpose: Master Component Registry v1.0 — 42 reusable building blocks + ACEI brand separation
builds.summary: 42 components across 7 categories; ACEI org identity; Citizens United as subject not brand; MC dashboard.
decisions_made: Organization: Arkansas Civic Education Initiative (ACEI)
Platform subject: The Citizens United Education Platform
ACUCOS renamed to ACEI Coalition System (legacy noted)
Build #18 recommended: Brand & Identity System before UI design
open_questions: Keep public site name Citizens Facts?

builds.title: Complete Page & Route Inventory
builds.purpose: Route Registry v1.0 — every screen and canonical path for Arkansas v1
builds.summary: 81 routes, 9 groups, Action Hub links, launch priorities, hub pages, netlify redirects.
decisions_made: Canonical paths redirect to current destinations until dedicated pages ship
Honest status: live / redirect / stub / planned per route
Pivot continues: Build #17 component inventory

builds.title: Arkansas Citizens United Platform Master Data Model & Relationship Architecture
builds.purpose: Canonical Data Constitution v1.0 — everything connected
builds.summary: 10 canonical objects, 20 relationship types, geographic/timeline/search intelligence, AI-ready schema, MC relationship health dashboard.
decisions_made: Single interconnected ecosystem — not isolated signup/content/coalition systems
Relationship growth as major success indicator
Pivot Builds #16–#20 to concrete product design (pages, components, DB, wireframes, deploy)
risks: 0 relationship edges until backend — honest scaffolding

builds.title: Arkansas Citizens United Coalition Operating System (ACUCOS)
builds.purpose: Coalition Architecture & Growth Blueprint v1.0 — statewide organizational backbone
builds.summary: ACUCOS: 6 participation levels, 7 categories, 75 county pages, growth engine, 4-metric dashboard, recognition & communications scaffold.
decisions_made: ACUCOS as coalition operating system — not a supporter list
One educational partner per county as long-term vision
6 participation levels including Observer and Event Partner
County completeness tracked in Mission Control
open_questions: Social API integration for outreach metrics?
risks: County pages and metrics at 0 — honest scaffolding

builds.title: Coalition Building & Public Outreach
builds.purpose: First-class Coalition & Outreach Layer — Arkansas educational coalition, not a political campaign
builds.summary: Coalition directory, 5 membership levels, org join form, resource center, event calendar, growth dashboard, three-path homepage.
decisions_made: Coalition as first-class platform layer alongside Education, Research, Civic Action
Homepage three parallel entry paths — Learn, Help, Partner
Organizations join educational coalition, not political campaign
County coalition map as table first; interactive map planned
open_questions: Public org directory listing vs. approval workflow?
Social media API integration for command center metrics?
Supabase for org profiles and event calendar?
risks: All coalition metrics at 0 until orgs join — honest scaffolding

builds.title: Arkansas Civic Action Ecosystem & Leadership Development System
builds.purpose: Arkansas Civic Participation Constitution — educate first, Arkansas pilot state
builds.summary: Arkansas Education Ladder (7 levels), 75-county participation table, Arkansas Action Hub, community conversation program, leadership dashboard, participant schema v1.6.
decisions_made: Arkansas as pilot state — prove model in 75 counties before expansion
7-level Arkansas Education Ladder governs hub action visibility
Education before action — min_level gates on all hub actions
County-level participation tracking for outreach planning
open_questions: Interactive Arkansas county map vs. table?
risks: Metrics at 0 until forms/backend — honest scaffolding

builds.title: Citizens United Knowledge Graph & Educational Intelligence Architecture
builds.purpose: Connected knowledge system — every object interrelated, Explore Further on every page
builds.summary: 8 object types, 38 nodes, 62 edges, 10 clusters, educational intelligence rules, Explore Further JS, Mission Control knowledge map.
decisions_made: KG-{TYPE}-{6-digit} permanent node IDs
Citizens United as hub node (KG-CASE-000003)
Explore Further auto-injected on all non-MC content pages
AI layer planned — must cite verified knowledge base only
risks: Graph completeness ~8% of v1 target — framework ahead of content depth

builds.title: Master Research & Evidence Framework
builds.purpose: Research Constitution — every claim traceable to Evidence IDs and reliable sources
builds.summary: 5 research principles, 5-tier source hierarchy, Evidence Registry (14 seeded), claim verification, review workflow, research dashboard.
decisions_made: EV-{6-digit} permanent Evidence IDs
Five-tier source hierarchy governs reliability
Claims ledger cross-linked to Evidence IDs
Academic sources flagged as research gap (0 cataloged)
open_questions: Auto-inject evidence panels on hall pages?
risks: Citation coverage still ~12% — framework ahead of content depth

builds.title: Visual Design System & Experience Blueprint
builds.purpose: Institutional design language — museum/library/archive aesthetic, not campaign site
builds.summary: Design tokens, 14 ds-* components, showcase page, emotional journey, domain colors, data viz standards, accessibility.
decisions_made: Domain accent colors organize information — never persuade
ds-* component prefix for governance
Mission Control dark theme distinct but connected
open_questions: Trust bar auto-inject on all hall pages in Build #10?
risks: Component adoption requires discipline in content builds

builds.title: User Experience Architecture & Journey System
builds.purpose: Design around people — Citizen Journey Blueprint with session memory and adaptive UX
builds.summary: 6 personas, 7-stage ladder, learning panel, continue-learning prompt, stage-aware Action Hub, success indicators.
decisions_made: localStorage session memory v1
Education before action — hub adapts by stage
No gamification — milestones encourage without points
open_questions: Server-side journey sync when backend exists?
risks: localStorage-only — journey resets on clear data

builds.title: Master Requirement ID System & Traceability Framework
builds.purpose: Permanent MRIDs for every platform component — Mission Control central nervous system
builds.summary: MRID v1.0: 165 requirements, 16 domains, traceability matrix, dependency lookup. 132 content items cross-linked.
decisions_made: 16 domain codes (GOV, NAV, HOME, CASE, etc.)
Content inventory IDs map to MRIDs — dual registry
12-step requirement lifecycle distinct from content status
open_questions: Display MRIDs on public pages in Build #8?
risks: 165 MRIDs of growing universe — regenerate discipline required

builds.title: Master Content Inventory
builds.purpose: Bill of materials for every educational asset — page-level tracking from day one
builds.summary: Content Master Registry v1.0: 351 items with stable IDs. 10 domains, cross-domain assets, 13-step lifecycle.
decisions_made: Stable ID standard (DOMAIN-NNN)
Nothing created without registry entry
L2-L4 depth slots pre-registered per subsection
open_questions: Which subsection gets first L2 draft in Build #7?
Timeline vs glossary priority for cross-domain?
risks: 351 registered of ~2700 v1 targets — discipline needed to expand registry as content grows

builds.title: Master Site Architecture & Navigation Blueprint
builds.purpose: Canonical map of every destination before individual page design
builds.summary: Master IA v1.0 with 10 primary sections, 82 subsections, reader journey, cross-linking standards, URL redirects, public /explore/ site map
decisions_made: Question-based primary nav
Canonical URLs with hall redirects during migration
Discover-to-Lead reader journey on all pages
open_questions: Which subsection gets first deep page in Build #6?
Search: static index vs live API?
risks: 76 planned subsections — content inventory must prioritize

builds.title: Master Phase Registry v1.0
builds.purpose: Governing 15-phase roadmap — every feature belongs to exactly one phase
builds.summary: Canonical phase registry with missions, deliverables, completion criteria, dependencies. 100 BUILD_PLAN steps mapped to registry phases. Full tracker in Mission Control.
decisions_made: 15-phase registry is governing roadmap; 100-step BUILD_PLAN is execution tracker
Honest per-phase completion — Phase 1 at 72%, not 100%
open_questions: Which hall gets L2 depth first — Hall 1 or Hall 2?
risks: Two phase systems require discipline to keep synchronized

builds.title: Mission Control Dashboard
builds.purpose: Project operating cockpit â€” track phases, steps, readiness, deployment, civic action
builds.summary: Static Mission Control at /mission-control with honest 3% baseline, progress bars, build map, public/admin views
decisions_made: Honest progress over inflated metrics
Static JSON v1, live APIs later
Public vs admin visibility levels
open_questions: When to add Supabase for participant profiles?
risks: Manual JSON updates may drift from reality without discipline

briefing.what_built: Facts Framework v1.0 — 13 canonical facts with evidence traceability
briefing.building_now: Content foundation — Brand & Identity System next (Build #19)
blocked: Hundreds of facts not yet cataloged
FEC spending data for FACT-4000 series

admin_only.internal_notes: Update site-architecture.json, phase-registry.json, and mission-control.json every commit.
